rule: The AI must never say an IV improves peptide efficacy unless direct human combination evidence exists.
required_language: supportive care only, based on patient symptom/lab/nutrition trigger

rule: Active peptide stack alone is not enough. The engine must require a patient-factor trigger.
blocked_language: because you are on peptides, you should get IV
required_language: if nausea/low intake/dehydration; if deficiency risk; if low protein/recovery phenotype; if provider-selected mitochondrial support

rule: Niagen/NR is nicotinamide riboside chloride, an NAD+ precursor, distinct from NAD+ IV.
required_language: NAD+ precursor; limited IV tolerability data; no peptide-combination evidence

rule: This stack/axis recommender does not provide doses or infusion rates. It routes to specific standing orders if the provider selects an IV option.
